scope_includes: insulation installation (batt and mineral wool only)

drywall_rules.measure_note: Each side of a wall can have different board — must track Side A and Side B separately
drywall_rules.optimization_note: AI should recommend the sheet size that produces least waste based on wall height

type_c.description: 5/8 inch Type C — enhanced fire rated

fire_rated_walls.detection_note: System MUST identify fire-rated walls — they multiply material quantities

1_hour.description: Single layer 5/8 Type X each side

2_hour.description: Two layers 5/8 Type X each side

vertical_carry.applies_when: multi-story AND no elevator or hoist access
vertical_carry.status: market test — adjust based on feedback

fiberglass_batt.note: Floor price — never bid below this. Adjust after getting real sub quotes.

mineral_wool.note: Floor price — never bid below this. Adjust after getting real sub quotes.

insulation.takeoff_note: SF comes from same wall type measurements as drywall — insulation specs listed in wall type detail

batt_takeoff.method: Wall batts: stud bays × vertical courses (wall height ÷ pre-cut batt length). Bays = stud_count − 1 from framing takeoff, else LF×12÷spacing. Then apply waste. Order using bags (FG) or bundles (mineral wool) from supplier counts.

fiberglass_batt.description: Example: 93" pre-cut batts; bag count varies by R-value and supplier

mineral_wool.description: Roxul-style batts; bundle sizes vary — verify with supplier

batt_takeoff.ceiling_note: Ceiling batts/rolls: rough pieces = ceiling SF ÷ nominal SF per piece (joist bay product — calibrate to your supplier)

gotchas.furring_channels: Flag when wall type mentions furring, resilient channels, or hat track — extra material and labor
gotchas.fire_caulking: Required at all penetrations through fire-rated assemblies — add as line item
gotchas.scissor_lift: Walls/ceilings above 12 ft — scissor lift rental (~$5K/mo KB); priced when wall height exceeds threshold
gotchas.mobilization: Same formula as framing when enabled on price_drywall (miles × $0.50 × days)
